mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning and depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the monthly unemployment jump fails to predict subsequent equity drawdowns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a sharp drop in residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant negative predictive relationship with 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction term loses significance after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting real GDP growth on equity valuations, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The negative predictive power of the FEDFUNDS-GDPC1 combination vanishes or flips to positive during historical tightening episodes, or forward returns do not differ significantly from baseline across regime splits.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue streams, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a year-over-year inflation rate of 3.5 percent, it signals overheating demand that forces aggressive monetary policy responses, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or high inflation readings fail to predict subsequent equity drawdowns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment rises while the unemployment rate simultaneously increases indicates a labor force participation surge rather than genuine job creation, which fails to support earnings growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce a statistically significant negative predictive relationship with 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction term loses significance after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in industrial production coupled with stabilizing real estate activity signals the bottoming of the business cycle, driving strongly positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of the INDPRO-HOUST rebound vanishes or flips to negative during historical recovery episodes, or forward returns do not differ significantly from baseline across regime splits.
